title: orchestration workflow schema (the steps model)
description: Defines the canonical shape of a workflow.yaml. The single root node is `workflow`; its `steps` constitute the flow. Steps form a flow with three shapes, all expressed through `after` preconditions: FORK = sibling steps sharing one `after`; MERGE = a step with several `after`s; ITERATION/LOOP = the acyclic step body re-executed per item at runtime — the `after` DAG stays acyclic (no graph cycles), the run log tolerates repeated step executions (replay = latest-line-wins, ordering uses first-occurrence), and the loop is bounded by state completion conditions. Each step's obligations are ONE flat `conditions` list. Every condition is `type: after` or `type: state`: `after` binds a predecessor `step_id`; `state` binds a `set_selector` + `set_predicate` — selection is artifact-backed (`artifact_types` + `set_query` → `selected`), and to assert on the acting unit the query filters by the `unit_id` runtime constant. Behavioral guidance lives at the step level (step.instructions / step.prompts). There is no per-workflow Python. Mediation is an implicit harness function — there is no `facilitate` step kind; participant→participant `after` edges are legal and the harness sequences them. See the harness README Logging section.

identifier.description: A lowercase, hyphen/underscore slug (workflow id, step id).

conditionId.description: A snake_case predicate id, unique within its step; it appears in the run log's conditions[] entries.

artifactTypeRef.description: One FROM binding for artifact selection: alias + schema id.

schema_id.description: Artifact schema identifier selected by this alias.

setSelector.description: Selector for a `type: state` condition. Selection is artifact-backed: `artifact_types` declares the FROM bindings (alias + schema_id) and `set_query` (CEL) yields the selected set, bound to the predicate as `selected`. To assert on the acting unit, filter by the `unit_id` runtime constant — e.g. `epic.filter(e, e.id == unit_id)`. Every `<alias>.<property>` reference is statically checked against the alias's schema — an undeclared property is a hard error.

artifact_types.description: FROM bindings for set selection (alias + schema_id).

set_query.description: CEL set query yielding the selected set, using only declared aliases plus the runtime constants (`unit_id`, `product`). Filter by `unit_id` to scope to the acting unit.

condition.description: One entry in a step's flat `conditions` list. `kind` = WHEN it holds (precondition / postcondition). `type` = WHAT it is: `after` (structural predecessor via `step_id`) or `state` (a portfolio/unit-state assertion via `set_selector` + `set_predicate`). Behavioral guidance lives at the step level (step.instructions / step.prompts).

kind.description: WHEN the condition holds: precondition (before the step acts) or postcondition (after).

type.description: WHAT the condition is about: `after` (structural predecessor via `step_id`) or `state` (a state assertion via `set_selector` + `set_predicate`).

id.description: Stable id, required on EVERY condition — the handle in the run log, findings, and check-step.

step_id.description: For `type: after`: predecessor step id that must be complete.

set_selector.description: For `type: state`: the artifact-backed selector (`artifact_types` + `set_query`) yielding the `selected` set to assert on. Filter by `unit_id` to assert on the acting unit.

set_predicate.description: For `type: state`: a CEL boolean over the `selected` set. Every `<alias>.<property>` reference is statically checked against the alias's schema — an undeclared property is a hard error.

step.description: One structurant step (one actor's turn). Its conditions are ONE flat `conditions` list using `type: after|state`: `after` binds `step_id` for structural dependencies, `state` binds `set_selector` + `set_predicate` for portfolio-state assertions. Behavioral guidance (instructions, prompts) is attached at the step level via step.instructions and step.prompts. Exactly one `actor` per step (one actor per turn): a turn taken by several participants is several forked steps, not one. NOTE: a step's own `kind` (author/challenge/delegate/render/gate/commit/verify/direct/…) is the step's nature and is unrelated to a condition's `kind` (precondition/postcondition).

gate.description: Human ★-gate label for a `kind: gate` step (e.g. '★ Epic Gate'). Authoring metadata naming which gate this step represents; the functional trigger is `kind: gate`.

delegates_to.description: For kind: delegate — the sub-workflow this step runs (path under workflows/).

skills.description: The ordered skill ids the dispatched agent loads for THIS step (per-step, not per-workflow). The harness injects them as deterministic context at sessionStart (inject-skills); a sub-orchestration is a step, so its procedure skill rides here.

output.description: The single artifact KIND this step produces or updates (effect-determinism, invariant 15). Resolves to a real, owned, typed artifact via the artifact catalog + ACL.

conditions.description: The step's flat obligations list — each condition is either an `after` dependency or a `state` assertion.

instructions.description: Step-level guidance injected to the agent at session-open (once preconditions pass). Carries behavioral guidance refs (e.g., authority obligations, discovery lenses). A single string or array of strings, each a contract/repo-relative ref to a `.instructions.md` file.

prompts.description: Step-level prompt guidance injected to the agent at session-open (once preconditions pass). A single string or array of strings, each a contract/repo-relative ref to a `.prompt.md` file.

workflow.description: The single root node. Carries the workflow header (`id`, `facilitator`, optional `kind`/`parent`) plus the `steps` that constitute it. Root workflows are identified by id ∈ {lpm, art, scrum} and carry no parent; sub-workflows declare kind/parent.

steps.description: The structurant steps constituting this workflow (one actor per step; fork/merge/loop via `after`).
